summary: A Go-based test orchestration framework with modular internal packages for assertions, schema validation, reporting, and MCP integration, developed using an agent-driven parallel workflow.
architecture: monolith (Go monorepo with modular internal packages)
patterns: test-driven development (extensive test suites across multiple agents)
agent-based orchestration (GOrchestra pattern for parallel task execution)
concerns: proliferation of agent directories (25+ agents) may indicate fragmented development without consolidation
truncated directory names (e.g., internalschema-validatio) suggest filesystem or tooling constraints
9 modified files with only 1 staged suggests incomplete or stalled work-in-progress
heavy test file count relative to source files may indicate over-testing or missing core implementation
scattered test files across multiple agent directories duplicate similar test patterns
suggestions: consolidate agent test directories into a unified test suite to reduce duplication
review and commit or stash the 9 modified files to maintain clean working state
document the GOrchestra agent orchestration pattern for contributor onboarding
evaluate if MCP handlers should be extracted into a separate package for clarity
add integration tests that validate cross-package interactions between runner, schema, and reporter
